src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-authentication-001 title: successful user registration with valid otp module: user registration source files or pages: 1, 3 covered methods: preconditions: - user is not registered in the system. - email service is functional. - database is accessible. test data: - username: `testuser123` - email: `testuser123@example.com` - password: `securepass123!` - otp: `123456` (simulated or generated by the system) test steps: 1. user opens the registration page and fills in the form with valid credentials. 2. system sends a registration request (`doregistration()`) to the server. 3. server saves user data (`salvataggio dati`) and generates an otp (`generateotp()`). 4. server sends otp via email (`mail otp`). 5. user enters the received otp (`digita codice otp`) in the app. 6. server validates the otp (`seridauthtoken()`) and confirms registration. expected result: - user account is created in the `user` table with hashed password. - otp is deleted or marked as used after validation. - user receives a success message and is redirected to the login page. unit: project:

src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-authentication-003 title: successful user login with valid credentials module: user login source files or pages: 1, 4 covered methods: preconditions: - user is registered (`user` table has an entry). - database is accessible. test data: - email: `testuser123@example.com` - password: `securepass123!` test steps: 1. user enters email and password in the login form. 2. app sends `sendauthtoken{}` to the server. 3. server retrieves the password hash from the database (`richiesta password associata alla mail`). 4. server verifies the password (`checkpassword()`). 5. server generates and saves an auth token (`generatetoken()`). 6. server sends the token back to the app (`salvaggio authtoken{}`). 7. app stores the token and opens the homepage. expected result: - user session is created in the `session` table. - auth token is stored in the app (e.g., local storage). - homepage loads successfully with user-specific data. unit: project:

src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-matching-001 title: successful match between two users module: user matching source files or pages: 1, 7 covered methods: preconditions: - user1 and user2 are registered and logged in. - both users have swiped right on each other’s cards. test data: test steps: 1. user1 swipes right on user2’s card (`swipe card dx utente 2`). 2. app1 sends `trackobjectpref(true)` to the server. 3. server saves the preference in the database (`salvataggio preferenza`). 4. server checks for matches (`checkmatch(false)` initially, then `checkmatch(true)` after mutual swipe). 5. user2 swipes right on user1’s card (`swipe card dx utente 1`). 6. server confirms the match and notifies app1. 7. app1 displays the match screen (`schermata match`). expected result: - both users see a match confirmation. - a `message` record is created (optional, for match notification). - push notification is triggered for both users (if enabled). unit: project:
